document.title: Risk Management Plan for B Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

sourceSummary.oneLine: Risk management plan for B Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector models B1417MCW, B1717MCW, FB1417MCW, and FB1717MCW.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Establishes and documents the manufacturer's risk management plan, responsibilities, risk acceptability criteria, residual risk evaluation, verification activities, and production/post-production information review activities.
keyPoints: The plan applies to all stages of the life cycle of B1417MCW, B1717MCW, FB1417MCW, and FB1717MCW digital flat panel detectors.
The product is classified as IIa and has an expected service life of approximately 5 years.
Risk acceptability is based on applicable national or regional regulations, relevant International Standards, state of the art, and stakeholder concerns.
Risk level is calculated as severity rating multiplied by probability rating.
Estimated risk levels 1 to 4 are acceptable, while levels 5 to 25 are unacceptable.

Production and post-production information is collected from internal sources such as PMCF, CAPA, complaints, service records, and surveys, and external sources such as incidents, literature, similar devices, and legal or standards trends.

concepts.evidence: The B1417MCW/B1717MCW/FB1417MCW/FB1717MCW are wired or wireless digital flat panel detectors that have been designed for faster, more streamlined approach to digital radiography systems.

evidence.claim: The document is a risk management plan for B Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector.
evidence.evidenceText: Risk Management Plan for B Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

evidence.claim: The document covers product models B1417MCW, B1717MCW, FB1417MCW, and FB1717MCW.
evidence.pageTarget: B Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

evidence.claim: The document revision is Rev.0 and was established on Oct 18, 2024.
evidence.evidenceText: Revision No. 0 Rev. Date Oct/18/2024 Revision Description Establishment

evidence.claim: The document was prepared, reviewed, and approved on Oct 18, 2024.
evidence.evidenceText: Prepared by Kim, Myeong seop R&D team Oct 18, 2024 Reviewed by Lee, Dong ho R&D Manager Oct 18, 2024 Approved by Hur, Nam kyu Quality Manager Oct 18, 2024

evidence.claim: The plan is intended to establish and document risk management in accordance with the risk management procedure.
evidence.evidenceText: This is a document for the H&abyz Co., Ltd. to establish and document a risk management plan in accordance with the risk management procedure.

evidence.claim: The plan applies to all life cycle stages of the listed detector models.
evidence.evidenceText: This Risk management plan is applicable to all stages of the life cycle of the B1417MCW/B17MCW/FB1417MCW/FB1717MCW.

evidence.claim: The device classification stated in the plan is IIa.
The expected service life stated in the plan is approximately 5 years.
The intended purpose is general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy for adults and children.
evidence.evidenceText: The B1417MCW/B1717MCW/FB1417MCW/FB1717MCW is indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children.

evidence.claim: Applicable regional regulation identified in the plan includes EU Regulation 2017/745.
evidence.evidenceText: European Union Regulation (EU) 2017/745 of the European Parliament and of the Council of 5 April 2017

evidence.claim: The plan references Korean medical device law for risk management report verification.
evidence.evidenceText: Risk management report Medical Device Act in Korea Implementing regulation in medical device in Korea

evidence.claim: Risk acceptability criteria are based on applicable regulations, international standards, state of the art, and stakeholder concerns.
evidence.evidenceText: This policy shall ensure that criteria are based upon applicable national or regional regulations and relevant International Standards, and take into account available information such as the generally accepted state of the art and known stakeholder concerns.

evidence.claim: Risk level is calculated as severity rating multiplied by probability rating.
evidence.evidenceText: Risk level = Severity rating × Probability rating

evidence.claim: Risk levels 1 to 4 are acceptable and risk levels 5 to 25 are unacceptable.
evidence.evidenceText: Risk Level: 1~4 Green, Acceptable Risk (A) Acceptable region Risk Level: 5~25 Red, Unacceptable Risk (U) Unacceptable region

evidence.claim: Overall residual risk must be evaluated after all risk control measures are implemented and verified.
evidence.evidenceText: After all risk control measures have been implemented and verified, the manufacturer shall evaluate the overall residual risk posed by the medical device...

evidence.claim: Post-production information includes PMCF, CAPA reports, customer complaints, service records, and customer satisfaction surveys.
evidence.evidenceText: Internal (1) PMCF (Post Market Clinical Follow up) (2) Corrective and preventive actions related reports (3) Customer Complaints (4) A / S recorded and maintained the information obtained during maintenance (5) Customer satisfaction surveys

evidence.claim: The Quality manager performs risk analysis for collected production and post-production information once every 12 months.
evidence.evidenceText: Risk analysis for the collected information (once every 12 months)

reviewFlags.reason: Source metadata authoritySignal is FDA, but the source text identifies European Union Regulation (EU) 2017/745 and Korean medical device regulation references; no FDA authority is present in the source text.
The scope section lists model B17MCW, while the title and product sections list B1717MCW; model evidence is internally inconsistent.
Preprocessed text contains OCR or extraction quality issues such as malformed punctuation, missing spaces, and inconsistent standard formatting.
